creator: jrm@freebsd.org
text:
Thanks for the report.

I ran the same test [1] with emacs-devel-30.0.50.20230101,3 [2] on 14.0-CURRENT main-n259729-69542f26820b GENERIC-NODEBUG amd64.  I don't get a complete freeze, but I do get a pause where input is ignored for a second or two, then everything is normal.  On an arch system running emacs-git 30.0.50.164336-1, I see a little hiccup, but it's not as long as on FreeBSD.

Could you confirm whether you mean a complete freeze as in Emacs is indefinitely unusable or a pause for a fixed time?

[1] emacs -Q -f view-hello-file

[2]
These are the options I built the emacs-devel package with:
        ACL            : on
        ALSA           : off
        CAIRO          : on
        DBUS           : off
        GFILE          : off
        GIF            : on
        GNUTLS         : on
        GSETTINGS      : off
        GTK2           : off
        GTK3           : on
        HARFBUZZ       : on
        JPEG           : on
        JSON           : on
        KQUEUE         : on
        LCMS2          : on
        M17N           : off
        MAGICK         : off
        MAILUTILS      : off
        MODULES        : on
        NATIVECOMP     : on
        OSS            : on
        OTF            : off
        PGTK           : off
        PNG            : on
        SCROLLBARS     : on
        SOURCES        : on
        SQLITE3        : on
        SVG            : on
        THREADS        : on
        TIFF           : on
        TREESITTER     : on
        WEBP           : on
        XAW            : off
        XAW3D          : off
        XFT            : off
        XIM            : on
        XML            : on
        XPM            : off
        XWIDGETS       : off
